target.name: Palantir
target.founder: Peter Thiel, Alex Karp, Stephen Cohen, Joe Lonsdale, Nathan Gettings

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $126.0B - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Palantir is a public-stage AI & Software company currently trading at $400B+. The valuation peer set comprises one public comparable (Palantir itself) and eight private frontier-tech peers, with the primary comparison universe consisting of five same-sector, similar-valuation-tier companies: OpenAI ($300.0B), xAI ($250.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), Databricks ($134.0B), and Waymo ($126.0B).

Peer-median triangulation is the applicable method given data availability. The median valuation across the five-peer cohort stands at $183.0B, with a range spanning $126.0B (Waymo floor) to $300.0B (OpenAI ceiling). This method yields an implied valuation of $183.0B. When applying a ±15% band around the median, the triangulated range is $155.6B to $210.4B.

The current $400B+ market capitalization sits materially above the peer-derived implied range, suggesting either a substantial Palantir-specific valuation premium or divergence in market pricing relative to the private frontier-tech cohort. Key uncertainties include whether public-market liquidity commands a meaningful multiple over private peer valuations, the degree to which Palantir's government contract revenue justifies premium positioning, and whether the private peer set adequately represents comparable AI & Software risk-return profiles. Single-method triangulation limits confidence; additional approaches (revenue multiple, discounted cash flow) would strengthen the analysis.
